desc.line_2: Capture content, shape context, and run agents on top of it.

Why.title: Why
Why.subtitle: Most AI loses context between sessions. Polywise keeps it structured, searchable, and yours.

What.title: What
What.subtitle: What is Polywise?
What.headline: Polywise is a local-first agentic content system.
What.lead: It helps you capture content, shape working context, and run agents against knowledge that stays under your control.

0.title: Local-first storage
0.desc: Content, memory, and agent state live on your machine first instead of starting in someone else’s cloud.

1.title: Scoped knowledge
1.desc: Store content globally, per project, or per agent so the same system can support both shared knowledge and focused runs.

2.title: Hybrid retrieval
2.desc: Combine keyword search, vectors, recall, and reranking in one pipeline to recover the right context before an agent acts.

3.title: Multi-agent work
3.desc: Run sessions for agents, groups, projects, and linked tasks instead of treating every workflow as a single chat thread.

4.title: Link capture
4.desc: Save URLs, extract articles, bind them to agents, and schedule recurring fetch jobs to keep source material fresh.

5.title: Bring your models
5.desc: Use remote providers, MCP tools, and local llama runtimes without locking the system to a single vendor.

6.title: Multiple surfaces
6.desc: Use Polywise through desktop sessions, API routes, scheduled jobs, and IM connectors while keeping one context system underneath.

Painpoint.title: Pain Points
Painpoint.subtitle: Why agentic work breaks without a real content system

0.title: Stateless AI
0.pain: Every new session starts cold, so you keep re-explaining the same work.
0.solution:
Polywise turns saved content into reusable context.
Agents can retrieve scoped knowledge instead of depending on whatever still fits in the current chat window.

1.title: Fragmented context
1.pain: Tasks, notes, links, projects, and agent prompts usually live in separate tools.
1.solution:
Polywise keeps content, sessions, todos, projects, and agents in one local system.
That makes context easier to connect, search, reuse, and automate.

2.title: Cloud lock-in
2.pain: Many AI workflows run on opaque systems you cannot inspect, migrate, or truly own.
2.solution:
Polywise keeps the runtime local-first and model-flexible.
You can bring providers, local models, and custom tools without giving up ownership of the underlying system.

Features.title: Features
Features.subtitle: Core capabilities for context, retrieval, and execution

0.title: Content Layer
0.desc:
Capture notes, tasks, links, and source material locally.
Keep content scoped to your global space, a project, or a specific agent.

1.title: Memory Retrieval
1.desc:
Blend keywords, vectors, recall, and reranking in one search pipeline.
Bring the right context back before an agent responds or acts.

2.title: Agent Sessions
2.desc:
Give each agent its own role, tools, model, and memory.
Run reusable sessions and move behavior between machines with agent packs.

3.title: Workflow Engine
3.desc:
Tie together projects, todos, sessions, groups, schedules, and IM hooks.
Turn saved context into repeatable execution instead of one-off chats.

0.a: Polywise is a local-first agentic content system. It helps you capture content, build reusable context, and run agents against that context instead of starting from an empty chat every time.

1.a: You can save content, organize it by project or agent, search it through the retrieval pipeline, and then run sessions, tasks, or scheduled jobs that use that context.

2.a: No. Writing is one use case, but the system is built for broader agentic work: research, task execution, workflow coordination, link capture, and long-running project context.

3.q: Do I need extra AI subscriptions to use Polywise?
3.a: Not always. What you need depends on which models or providers you want to run. Polywise is designed to work with different providers rather than forcing one billing path.

4.q: Can I use my existing model providers with Polywise?
4.a: Yes. Polywise is built around provider flexibility, so you can connect the model stack that already fits your workflow instead of switching everything to a bundled vendor.

5.a: Yes. Polywise includes support for local runtimes such as llama-based setups, so part of the system can stay fully on your machine when you want tighter control.

6.a: Polywise uses a hybrid retrieval pipeline. It combines keywords, vectors, recall, and reranking so agents can recover relevant context instead of relying on a single search method.

7.a: Polywise is local-first. Your content, memory, and runtime state can remain under your control, which makes backups, inspection, and privacy boundaries much clearer than cloud-only systems.

8.a: Yes. Polywise includes sessions, todos, schedules, link fetch jobs, and connector surfaces that can be composed into recurring workflows instead of manual one-off runs.

9.a: Yes. Polywise is available on GitHub, which means you can inspect the code, track changes, and understand the system you are building your workflow on.
